title: Design WhatsApp
overview: Build a messaging platform supporting 1-on-1 chats, group messaging, message delivery guarantees, and media sharing — all at global scale.

levels.title: Basic Messaging
levels.constraints: 10K users. Send text messages between two users. Messages must be stored so offline users receive them when they reconnect. No real-time requirement.
levels.feedbackHint: Good foundation! Polling the DB for new messages on every client refresh is very inefficient. How do you push messages instantly to online users?
levels.aiContextHint: Level 1 is a basic store-and-forward design. Messages are stored in a DB. Recipients poll for new messages. Accept any server + database design.

levels.title: Real-time Delivery
levels.constraints: 1M users. Polling causes massive DB load and 10–30s message delays. Users expect instant delivery when both parties are online.
levels.feedbackHint: WebSocket connections (maintained via server + cache for session state) enable instant push delivery. The cache stores active connection metadata. Messages still go to DB for offline users.
levels.aiContextHint: Level 2 adds persistent WebSocket connections between clients and servers. The cache stores which server holds each user's connection. When a message arrives, the server looks up the recipient's connection and pushes immediately. Offline users get messages from DB on reconnect.

levels.title: Media Messages
levels.constraints: Users send photos, videos, and voice notes. Uploading 50MB videos directly through the chat server causes timeouts and saturates server bandwidth.
levels.feedbackHint: Direct-to-CDN uploads bypass the chat server entirely. The server only stores the media URL and sends it as a message. Recipients download media from the CDN edge node near them.
levels.aiContextHint: Level 3 uses pre-signed CDN upload URLs. Clients upload media directly to CDN/object storage without going through the chat server. The server receives only the media URL and routes it as a message. CDN serves media to recipients from the nearest edge node.

levels.title: Group Messaging
levels.constraints: Groups can have up to 1,000 members. Sending a message to a 1,000-member group requires 1,000 WebSocket pushes — the server blocks on fan-out for seconds.
levels.feedbackHint: Async fan-out via a message queue (NoSQL) decouples the sender response from delivery to all members. Workers drain the queue and push to each member's WebSocket connection.
levels.aiContextHint: Level 4 uses an async fan-out queue (NoSQL) for group messages. The server enqueues a delivery job and immediately returns a 'sent' acknowledgement. Worker services dequeue jobs and fan out to each group member's connection server.

levels.title: End-to-End Delivery Guarantees
levels.constraints: Messages are sometimes lost when servers restart or a recipient reconnects to a different server. Users need 'delivered' and 'read' receipts with guaranteed exactly-once delivery.
levels.feedbackHint: You've designed a production-grade messaging system! Message sequence numbers in the DB, client-side ACKs, and a durable queue ensure exactly-once delivery. The Reverse Proxy handles sticky WebSocket routing to the correct server node.
levels.aiContextHint: Level 5 adds delivery guarantees: monotonic message sequence numbers per conversation (RDBMS), client ACK protocol, and a durable queue (NoSQL) that retains messages until ACK'd. The Reverse Proxy routes WebSocket reconnections to the server holding the existing session (sticky sessions).
